verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 encodes a tetrameric IP3-gated calcium-release channel resident in the ER/sarcoplasmic reticulum membrane. Its topology places all functional domains (N-terminal IP3-binding domain, regulatory domains, C-terminal channel pore) on the cytoplasmic face or within the ER lumen, with no extracellular-facing domain at the plasma membrane outer leaflet. It is not GPI-anchored, has no signal peptide for classical secretion, and is not a plasma-membrane receptor. Pre-no checklist: no documented cancer/cell-death outer-leaflet display for ITPR3 specifically; no germline/tissue-restricted PM pool; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented minority PM pool or secretory cycling; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to a surface scaffold. No CD designation and HGNC group 'IP3 receptors' refers to intracellular ER calcium channels. No clinical surface-directed therapeutic program targets ITPR3 on intact cell surfaces.
